rulings.Case Title: CESAR ASCARRUNZ VS. HOWARD HSU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney Fees And Costs Date: August 18, 2014 Time: 9:30 A.M. Location: Dept. 302 (Reservation #070814-04) Civil Codeâ§ 1717
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, September 3, 2014, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT MILLIE LUONG'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney Fees And Costs Date: August 18, 2014 Time: 9:30 A.M. Location: Dept. 302 (Reservation #070814-04) Civil Code 1717 The motion is continued to September 26, 2014, to give Mr. Ascarrunz an ability to respond to Ms. Luong's reliance on Reynolds Metals Co. v. Alperson (1979) 25 Cal.3d 124, 129. Ms. Luong cited that decision for the first time in the reply brief. Mr. Ascarrunz may file a brief no more than 7 pages addressing: (1) whether that authority applies because par. 39 of the Third Amended Complaint has alter ego allegations and defendant Luong was named on the breach of contract cause of action, (3rd Amended Complaint, 26:13); and (2) the propriety of awarding fees pursuant to CC 1717 even though the litigation of the cross-complaint is ongoing. Mr. Ascarrunz's brief is due by 9/16/14. Ms. Luong may file a 3 page rebuttal brief by 9/22/14. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG

rulings.Case Title: CESAR ASCARRUNZ VS. HOWARD HSU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, September 3, 2014, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT MILLIE LUONG'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings The motion is denied. Ms. Luong fails to demonstrate that Mr. Ascarrunz's cross-complaint is time barred. "The contention cannot be sustained for the reason that the filing of the complaint in this action suspended the running of the statute of limitations as to matters arising out of the transaction pleaded." McDougald v. Hulet, (1901) 132 Cal. 154, 160-161. The motion is denied as to the declaratory relief cause of action because the claim pleads a live controversy regarding whether Ms. Luong endorsed the check. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG

rulings.Case Title: JAMES W. GAY VS. CREATIVE ALLIANCE, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILTY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Danilo Design Group, Telegraph Hill Properties Inc. And Wb Coyle'S Motion For A Protective Order Regarding Depositions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 3, 2014, Line 3, Defendants Danilo Design Group, Telegraph Hill Properties Inc. And Wb Coyle'S Motion For A Protective Order Regarding Depositions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion for a protective order regarding WB Coyle is denied. The earlier testimony of WB Coyle in his capacity as a party in a related arbitration proceeding is of no force or effect in the within action especially since he is being deposed as the person most knowledgeable for the Danilo Design Group and Telephone Hill Properties. There is no statutory time limitation for the deposition of a person most knowledgeable. See CCP 2025.290(b)(5). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES W. GAY VS. CREATIVE ALLIANCE, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILTY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Danilo Design Group, Telegraph Hill Properties Inc. And Wb Coyle'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff James Gay To Appear And Testify At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 3, 2014, Line 3, Defendants Danilo Design Group, Telegraph Hill Properties Inc. And Wb Coyle'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff James Gay To Appear And Testify At Deposition. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to set deposition of plaintiff, James Gay, is granted. The parties are directed to meet and confer prior to the hearing to agree to scheduling of the depositions of James Gay as well as the deposition of the person most knowledgeable for the Danilo Design Group and Telephone Hill Properties. The agreed dates shall be reflected in their proposed Order to be drafted by the moving party. If the parties are unable to agree to deposition dates, a hearing is required and the Court will set all depositions at that time. See the decision below with respect to the defendant's motion for a protective order which has been denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ELVIA JUAREZ VS. ANTHONY E NOVAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Discovery Sanctions Against Edward L. Novak
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, September 3, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF ELVIA JUAREZ' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Discovery Sanctions Against Edward L. Novak Plaintiff has demonstrated that Defendant Edward Novak has willfully failed to comply with four prior court orders against him. On June 9, 2014, the Court ordered Defendant to answer deposition questions and produce documents. Defendant has failed to comply with this order. On April 7, 2014, the Court ordered Defendant to answer interrogatories and pay monetary sanctions. Defendant has failed to comply with this order. On April 7, 2014, the Court ordered Defendant to respond to Plaintiff's Request for Production and pay monetary sanctions. Defendant has failed to comply with this order. On April 7, 2014, Plaintiff's Request for Admissions were deemed admitted and Defendant was ordered to pay monetary sanctions. Defendant has failed to comply with this order. Pursuant to CCP sec. 2023.030, the Court grants Plaintiff's motion for terminating sanctions. Defendant's answer is hereby stricken and default is entered against Defendant. Plaintiff shall prepare a form of judgment to bring to the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG

rulings.Case Title: ELVIA JUAREZ VS. ANTHONY E NOVAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Discovery Sanctions Against Defendant Anthony E. Novak
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, September 3, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF ELVIA JUAREZ' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Discovery Sanctions Against Defendant Anthony E. Novak Plaintiff has demonstrated that Defendant Anthony Novak has willfully failed to comply with seven prior court orders against him. On February 13, 2014, the Court ordered Defendant to respond to form interrogatories, respond to requests for production, and pay monetary sanctions. The Court also ordered Plaintiff's Requests for Admissions deemed admitted and Defendant was ordered to pay monetary sanctions. Defendant has failed to comply with these three orders. On April 7, 2014, the Court ordered Defendant to respond to interrogatories, respond to requests for production, and pay monetary sanctions. The Court also ordered Plaintiff's Requests for Admissions deemed admitted and Defendant was ordered to pay monetary sanctions. Defendant has failed to comply with these three orders. On May 22, 2014, the Court ordered Defendant to produce documents and pay monetary sanctions. Defendant has failed to comply with this order. Pursuant to CCP sec. 2023.030, the Court grants Plaintiff's motion for terminating sanctions. Defendant's answer is hereby stricken and default is entered against Defendant. Plaintiff shall prepare a form of judgment to bring to the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG
